days.label: Depart Chicago
notes: Flight departs 4:20 PM CDT, arrives 8:15 AM+1 CEST

activities.name: Schonbrunn Palace
activities.baby_notes: Stroller accessible via side entrance, elevator inside palace, huge flat gardens perfect for stroller walks, baby changing facilities available
activities.description: Imperial summer residence with 1,441 rooms, stunning Baroque gardens, and the world's oldest zoo next door

activities.name: St. Stephen's Cathedral (Stephansdom)
activities.baby_notes: Ground floor fully stroller accessible, skip the narrow south tower stairs with baby, north tower has elevator
activities.description: Vienna's iconic Gothic cathedral with its colorful tiled roof, the heart of the city since the 12th century

activities.name: Belvedere Palace
activities.baby_notes: Elevator access in Upper Belvedere, strollers allowed inside, large flat gardens between Upper and Lower palaces ideal for walks
activities.description: Baroque palace complex housing Klimt's The Kiss, with magnificent gardens offering city views

activities.name: Naschmarkt
activities.baby_notes: Flat outdoor market, wide walkways for strollers on weekday mornings, can get crowded on Saturdays -- go early
activities.description: Vienna's most famous open-air market with 120+ stalls selling international foods, spices, cheese, and produce

activities.name: Prater Park & Riesenrad
activities.baby_notes: Giant ferris wheel cabin is stroller accessible, Prater park has wide flat paths, shaded areas, and the Hauptallee is perfect for a long stroller walk
activities.description: Iconic amusement park with the 1897 giant ferris wheel, plus a vast green park with tree-lined avenues

restaurants.name: Figlmuller
restaurants.baby_notes: High chairs available, the Backerstrasse location at #6 has more spacious seating and is less cramped than Wollzeile for strollers
restaurants.description: Vienna's most famous schnitzel -- plate-sized and perfectly golden, a must-visit institution since 1905

restaurants.name: Cafe Central (at DECENTRAL during renovation)
restaurants.address: Freyung (DECENTRAL pop-up), 1010 Wien (main location Herrengasse 14 closed for renovation until autumn 2026)
restaurants.hours: 8:00-20:00 daily (check current hours for DECENTRAL pop-up)
restaurants.baby_notes: NOTE: Cafe Central main location is closed for renovation March-Autumn 2026. Visit DECENTRAL at Freyung instead -- same quality coffeehouse experience in a modern space
restaurants.description: Legendary coffeehouse where Freud and Trotsky once sat -- temporarily at DECENTRAL pop-up during 2026 renovation

restaurants.name: Cafe Sacher
restaurants.baby_notes: Spacious interior, high chairs available on request, stroller can stay tableside, go early morning (before 9am) to avoid tourist queues
restaurants.description: Home of the original Sachertorte since 1832, an opulent coffeehouse opposite the Vienna State Opera

restaurants.name: Demel
restaurants.baby_notes: Can be tight with a full stroller -- consider baby carrier, staff accommodating, no reservations so go off-peak, beautiful display cases mesmerize babies
restaurants.description: Imperial court confectioner since 1786 with legendary pastries, cakes, and an ornate Baroque interior

restaurants.name: Cafe Sperl
restaurants.baby_notes: Spacious booths and tables with room for stroller, relaxed old-world atmosphere, less touristy than Sacher or Central, near Naschmarkt
restaurants.description: Beautifully preserved 1880 coffeehouse with billiard tables, newspapers, and authentic fin-de-siecle atmosphere

activities.name: Stadtpark
activities.baby_notes: Fully paved paths ideal for strollers even in rain, fenced toddler playground with sandpit and swings, benches throughout for nursing breaks
activities.description: Vienna's first public park with the golden Johann Strauss statue, shady walkways, and a children's playground

activities.name: Kunsthistorisches Museum
activities.baby_notes: Stroller entry via side entrance at Burgring 5 with staff escort to elevator, baby free admission, cloakroom stores strollers if needed
activities.description: World-class art museum with masterpieces by Bruegel, Vermeer, Raphael, and the Habsburg art collection

activities.name: Haus der Musik
activities.baby_notes: Barrier-free entrance, elevator to all floors, strollers welcome or can be stored at cloakroom, accessible restroom on ground floor
activities.description: Interactive sound museum where you can conduct a virtual orchestra and explore music through hands-on exhibits

activities.name: Schmetterlinghaus (Butterfly House)
activities.baby_notes: Small space but stroller navigable, warm tropical climate inside -- dress baby lightly, visually stimulating for infants with hundreds of free-flying butterflies
activities.description: Art Nouveau greenhouse in the Burggarten filled with hundreds of tropical butterflies from around the world

activities.name: Burggarten
activities.baby_notes: Flat manicured lawns perfect for a blanket and tummy time, paved paths for strollers, shady benches, Mozart statue nearby
activities.description: Elegant imperial garden behind the Hofburg with lawns, the Mozart monument, and the Palmenhaus cafe

restaurants.name: Plachutta Wollzeile
restaurants.baby_notes: High chairs provided, spacious dining room and winter garden section, staff very accommodating to families, stroller storage available
restaurants.description: The temple of Tafelspitz -- Vienna's definitive boiled beef, served in copper pots with traditional sides

restaurants.name: Gasthaus Poschl
restaurants.baby_notes: Small and cozy -- book ahead and request a corner table, high chairs available, authentic local crowd, less stroller-friendly so baby carrier recommended
restaurants.description: Beloved neighborhood Gasthaus with outstanding schnitzel, Tafelspitz, and Kaiserschmarrn -- a locals' favorite

restaurants.name: Luftburg - Kolarik im Prater
restaurants.baby_notes: Extremely family-friendly: huge beer garden, 1000m2 bouncy castle adventure world for older kids, high chairs, indoor kids room, changing facilities, wide paths for strollers
restaurants.description: World's largest organic restaurant in the Prater -- legendary pork knuckle, ribs, and schnitzel in a massive beer garden

restaurants.name: Vollpension
restaurants.baby_notes: Stroller-friendly with wide layout, welcoming atmosphere run by seniors who love babies, changing facilities, near Naschmarkt for a combined outing
restaurants.description: Generational cafe where grandmas bake -- traditional cakes, brunch, and heartwarming intergenerational concept

restaurants.name: Palmenhaus
restaurants.baby_notes: Gorgeous Art Nouveau glass house, spacious interior, stroller fits easily, outdoor terrace in Burggarten garden perfect for families in May weather
restaurants.description: Stunning Art Nouveau greenhouse brasserie in the Burggarten -- brunch, lunch, or cocktails in a botanical setting

activities.name: Karlskirche (St. Charles's Church)
activities.baby_notes: Ground floor accessible with stroller, panoramic elevator inside takes you to dome frescoes -- baby carrier recommended for upper level, reflecting pool outside is lovely
activities.description: Magnificent Baroque church with a panoramic elevator to the dome frescoes, fronted by a beautiful reflecting pool

activities.name: Danube Canal Walk
activities.baby_notes: Flat paved promenade ideal for strollers, vibrant street art to look at, several cafes along the canal for feeding/nursing stops
activities.description: Scenic waterside promenade with street art, pop-up bars, and views of Vienna -- the city's creative hub

activities.name: Augarten Park
activities.baby_notes: Flat gravel and paved paths throughout, four playgrounds including toddler area, quiet and uncrowded compared to Stadtpark -- great for a peaceful stroller walk
activities.description: Vienna's oldest Baroque park with chestnut-lined avenues, playgrounds, the Porcelain Manufactory, and far fewer tourists

activities.name: Ringstrasse Tram Ride (Tram 1 or 2)
activities.baby_notes: Low-floor trams with stroller space, baby rides free, a relaxing sightseeing loop past Opera, Parliament, Rathaus, Hofburg -- great if baby needs a nap on the go
activities.description: Ride the regular tram along the grand Ringstrasse boulevard past all major monuments -- a budget sightseeing tour

activities.name: Viktor-Adler-Markt (Favoriten local market)
activities.baby_notes: Outdoor market with easy stroller access, close to your Favoriten accommodation, authentic local experience with Turkish bakeries and fresh produce
activities.description: Multicultural neighborhood market in your home district -- Turkish bakeries, fresh produce, and local everyday Vienna

restaurants.name: Mayer am Pfarrplatz
restaurants.baby_notes: Large garden with outdoor seating ideal for families, relaxed atmosphere, stroller easy in garden area, Beethoven once lived here -- a special evening out
restaurants.description: Historic wine tavern where Beethoven composed, set in vineyard hills with own-grown wines and traditional cold buffet

restaurants.name: Schweizerhaus
restaurants.baby_notes: Huge outdoor beer garden with ample stroller space, casual atmosphere, Budvar on tap, famous Stelze (pork knuckle) -- combine with a Prater park visit
restaurants.description: Legendary Prater beer garden since 1920 known for crispy Stelze and Budweiser Budvar on tap

restaurants.name: Motto am Fluss
restaurants.baby_notes: Spacious modern interior, river terrace with views, high chairs available, relaxed daytime cafe on upper deck great for lunch with baby
restaurants.description: Sleek floating restaurant on the Danube Canal with panoramic windows, serving modern Austrian cuisine and great breakfast

restaurants.name: Glacis Beisl
restaurants.baby_notes: Beautiful tree-shaded garden terrace next to MuseumsQuartier, stroller-friendly outdoor area, high chairs, relaxed family vibe at lunch
restaurants.description: Charming garden restaurant tucked beside the MuseumsQuartier with Austrian classics and a shaded courtyard terrace

restaurants.name: Bitzinger Wurststand (Albertina)
restaurants.baby_notes: Outdoor standing tables only -- no seating, but quick and easy, eat by the Opera House, good for a fast bite when baby is in the carrier or stroller
restaurants.description: Vienna's most upscale sausage stand where opera-goers in tuxedos eat Kasekrainer beside the Albertina -- a quintessential Vienna experience

days.label: Vienna → Crete

car_rental.notes: Pickup details TBD - verify on Expedia.com

notes: Pick up rental car at airport

accommodation.name: Daios Cove Luxury Resort & Villas
accommodation.room: Deluxe Sea View w/ Individual Pool
accommodation.dining_notes: Baby puree available at all restaurants and Mini Market

activities.name: Spinalonga Island
activities.cost: EUR 20 entry + EUR 12 boat from Elounda
activities.baby_notes: Boat ride ~20 min each way; use baby carrier on island as paths are uneven cobblestone; no stroller access. Shaded areas limited, bring sun hat.
activities.description: Venetian fortress and former leper colony on a small island opposite Elounda. Fascinating history with well-preserved buildings and fortifications.

activities.name: Voulisma (Golden) Beach
activities.baby_notes: Excellent for babies: very shallow warm water extends far out, fine soft sand, gentle entry. Sunbed umbrellas provide shade. Parking lot right above beach. ~20 min drive from Daios Cove.
activities.description: Award-winning beach with Caribbean-like turquoise water and fine golden sand. One of the best family beaches in eastern Crete.

activities.name: Almyros Beach
activities.baby_notes: Extremely shallow calm water perfect for babies. Has a playground on the beach. Freshwater river meets the sea creating cool spots. Wide sandy area for crawling/sitting. Free roadside parking. ~10 min drive from Daios Cove.
activities.description: Popular sandy beach south of Agios Nikolaos with a unique freshwater river flowing into the sea. Shallow, calm, and well-organized with lifeguards.

activities.name: Kolokytha Beach
activities.baby_notes: Requires a 30-min walk on a rough dirt path from parking at Church of Agios Loukas. Not stroller accessible. Better for older children. Beautiful but remote.
activities.description: Secluded beach on the eastern side of the Spinalonga peninsula with crystal-clear water and wild natural beauty. Reached by foot or boat.

restaurants.name: Pangea (Daios Cove)
restaurants.baby_notes: Main resort restaurant. High chairs available. Buffet format lets you eat quickly if baby is fussy. Wide food variety including plain options for picky eaters.
restaurants.description: Daios Cove's main dining venue with lavish breakfast and dinner buffet featuring global cuisines, pasta bar, fresh seafood, and themed evening events.

restaurants.name: Ocean Club (Daios Cove)
restaurants.baby_notes: More upscale evening dining. High chairs available. Pool-view terrace is pleasant with baby. Creative Italian cuisine in collaboration with Ducasse Conseil.
restaurants.description: Elegant poolside restaurant serving creative Italian cuisine, transforming from a daytime breakfast spot into a refined evening dining experience.

restaurants.name: Beach House (Daios Cove)
restaurants.address: Daios Cove Luxury Resort, Beach Level, Vathi
restaurants.baby_notes: Most baby-friendly resort option. Casual beachside cabana setting. No need to dress up. Fresh juices and light healthy dishes. Feet-in-sand vibe.
restaurants.description: Rustic open-air beachfront restaurant serving seasonally-inspired light bites, salads, grilled fish, and creative cocktails from beach cabanas.

restaurants.name: Taverna (Daios Cove)
restaurants.baby_notes: Water's edge setting. Local Cretan specialties. High chairs available. Relaxed taverna atmosphere within the resort. Daily fresh catch.
restaurants.description: Resort taverna at the water's edge serving authentic Cretan specialties, fresh daily catch, and local wines in a traditional atmosphere.

accommodation.name: Daios Cove Luxury Resort & Villas
accommodation.room: Deluxe Sea View w/ Individual Pool
accommodation.dining_notes: Baby puree available at all restaurants and Mini Market

activities.name: Elounda Beach (Town Beach)
activities.baby_notes: Blue Flag beach right in town center. Shallow, protected water. Easy stroller access along the promenade. Restaurants and shops steps away for feeding/changing. ~5 min drive from Daios Cove.
activities.description: Blue Flag-rated beach in the center of Elounda with calm protected waters and full amenities. Walking distance to tavernas and shops.

activities.name: Havania Beach
activities.address: Elounda Road, north of Agios Nikolaos
activities.baby_notes: Blue Flag beach with lifeguards, calm shallow water, and a canteen. Easy access with ample parking. Some pebbles near shore - bring water shoes. ~8 min drive from Daios Cove.
activities.description: Organized municipal beach between Agios Nikolaos and Elounda with clear calm water and Blue Flag certification. Well-equipped with facilities.

activities.name: Daios Cove Private Beach
activities.baby_notes: Resort's own sandy beach with calm bay. Sunbeds and umbrellas included. Beach House restaurant on-site for refreshments. Easiest option with infant - no driving required.
activities.description: The resort's private sandy cove with crystal-clear water, full service, and the Beach House restaurant for light bites and cocktails.

activities.name: Elounda Harbour & Old Town Walk
activities.baby_notes: Flat waterfront promenade is stroller-friendly. Plenty of cafes for breaks. Fishing boats and harbor are visually engaging for baby. Shaded areas available at cafes.
activities.description: Stroll the picturesque fishing harbour, browse shops, and enjoy waterfront cafes in the charming village of Elounda.

restaurants.name: Ferryman Taverna
restaurants.baby_notes: Waterfront terrace with sea views. Relaxed atmosphere. Popular with families. Fresh grilled fish and Cretan classics. Book ahead in high season.
restaurants.description: Iconic Elounda taverna made famous by the TV series 'Who Pays the Ferryman.' Seaside setting with excellent fresh fish and warm hospitality.

restaurants.name: Olondi Restaurant
restaurants.baby_notes: Outdoor terrace overlooking Elounda Bay and Spinalonga. Steps from the sea. Relaxed family atmosphere. Known for generous fish platters and classic Cretan dishes.
restaurants.description: Elounda institution since 1990, famous for its fish platters and bay views. Combines excellent seafood with Cretan hospitality and stunning Spinalonga views.

restaurants.name: Kanali Restaurant
restaurants.baby_notes: Beautiful bay-view setting. Outdoor seating with gentle breeze. Spacious enough for a stroller. House salad and octopus are highlights.
restaurants.description: Waterfront restaurant overlooking Elounda Bay known for its extensive seafood menu, grilled octopus, and the house Kanali salad.

restaurants.name: Babel Restaurant
restaurants.baby_notes: Central square location with outdoor seating. Great people-watching spot. Good for a casual meal or coffee break. Stroller-friendly area.
restaurants.description: Popular cafe-restaurant on Elounda's main square with views of the harbour. Serves breakfast through dinner with Greek and Mediterranean dishes.

accommodation.name: Daios Cove Luxury Resort & Villas
accommodation.room: Deluxe Sea View w/ Individual Pool
accommodation.dining_notes: Baby puree available at all restaurants and Mini Market

activities.name: Lake Voulismeni & Agios Nikolaos Town
activities.baby_notes: Flat walkways around the lake are stroller-friendly. Many cafes with shaded outdoor seating line the lakeside. Small playground near the marina. ~15 min drive from Daios Cove.
activities.description: Explore the famous bottomless lake connected to the sea by a canal, surrounded by cafes and restaurants. The lively town has shops, a marina, and a small archaeological museum.

activities.name: Plaka Village
activities.baby_notes: Tiny charming village, mostly flat along the waterfront. Tavernas have outdoor seating perfect for lunch with a baby. Views of Spinalonga from the harbor.
activities.description: Small traditional fishing village opposite Spinalonga Island. Known for excellent seafood tavernas on the waterfront and a relaxed atmosphere.

activities.name: Sunken City of Olous
activities.baby_notes: Flat walk along the causeway. Can see ancient ruins in shallow water from the path. Baby carrier or stroller both work on the paved road.
activities.description: Walk along the canal causeway to see the remains of the ancient Minoan-Roman city of Olous submerged in the shallow waters. Visible mosaics and walls.

activities.name: Kritsa Village
activities.baby_notes: Narrow lanes with some steps - baby carrier recommended over stroller. Shaded alleys are pleasant. Known for handmade textiles and crafts. ~20 min drive from Daios Cove.
activities.description: One of the oldest and most picturesque villages in Crete, perched on a hillside. Famous for traditional textiles, crafts, excellent olive oil, and Venetian-era architecture.

restaurants.name: Vritomartes Restaurant
restaurants.baby_notes: Harbourside taverna with outdoor tables. Traditional Cretan dishes at reasonable prices. Family-run with welcoming atmosphere.
restaurants.description: Traditional harbourside taverna in Elounda serving authentic Cretan cuisine with fresh ingredients and reasonable prices.

restaurants.name: Ergospasio
restaurants.baby_notes: Atmospheric converted factory above the sea. Outdoor terrace with sunset views. More upscale but relaxed. Good for a special dinner out with baby.
restaurants.description: Stylish restaurant in a converted carob processing factory above the sea, serving creative Mediterranean cuisine with stunning sunset views.

restaurants.name: Giorgos Fish Tavern
restaurants.baby_notes: Waterfront tables on the rocks right by the sea. Very relaxed village atmosphere. Fresh catch of the day. Views of Spinalonga. Combine with Plaka village walk.
restaurants.description: Traditional fish tavern set on the sea rocks of Plaka harbour, overlooking Spinalonga Island. Famous for the freshest seafood in the area.

restaurants.name: Taverna Manolis (Plaka)
restaurants.baby_notes: Family-run with warm hospitality. Outdoor waterfront seating. Simple, fresh Cretan food. Relaxed pace perfect for dining with an infant.
restaurants.description: Family-run waterfront taverna in Plaka serving honest Cretan home cooking, fresh grilled fish, and local wines with Spinalonga views.

accommodation.name: Daios Cove Luxury Resort & Villas
accommodation.room: Deluxe Sea View w/ Individual Pool
accommodation.dining_notes: Baby puree available at all restaurants and Mini Market

activities.name: Panagia Kera Byzantine Church
activities.baby_notes: Small church, quick visit. Keep baby quiet inside. Shaded olive trees outside. Combine with Kritsa village visit. ~20 min drive from Daios Cove.
activities.description: 13th-century Byzantine church with some of the finest and best-preserved frescoes in Crete. A small but culturally significant site.

activities.name: Cretan Olive Oil Farm Tour
activities.baby_notes: Outdoor farm setting, relaxed pace. Baby can be in carrier. Olive oil and raki tasting for adults. Some farms have shaded seating areas. Call ahead to confirm baby-friendliness.
activities.description: Visit a working olive farm to learn about Cretan olive oil production, walk through ancient olive groves, and taste local olive oil, honey, and raki.

activities.name: Elounda Salt Pans & Windmills Walk
activities.baby_notes: Flat dirt road past old salt pans and stone windmills. Stroller possible but rough in spots - carrier is better. Beautiful views of Spinalonga.
activities.description: Walk past historic Venetian salt pans and ruined windmills on the road to the Spinalonga peninsula. Scenic photo opportunities with views of the fortress.

activities.name: Lassithi Plateau & Dikti Cave (Cave of Zeus)
activities.baby_notes: The cave descent has steep slippery steps - not suitable with a baby. However, the plateau drive itself is beautiful and you could enjoy the villages and scenery without entering the cave. ~50 min drive from Daios Cove.
activities.description: Dramatic mountain plateau at 850m elevation ringed by mountains. The mythological birthplace of Zeus is in the Dikteon Cave. Scenic drive through mountain villages.

restaurants.name: Gioma Meze
restaurants.baby_notes: Terrace with stunning lake and sea views. Traditional meze sharing plates work well for flexible dining with baby. Romantic ambiance in the evening.
restaurants.description: Atmospheric meze restaurant perched above Lake Voulismeni with panoramic views. Serves traditional Cretan small plates and sharing dishes.

restaurants.name: Karnagio (Pita tou Riga)
restaurants.baby_notes: Lakeside outdoor seating. Uses local Cretan ingredients. Known for excellent pitas and traditional dishes. Casual family-friendly atmosphere.
restaurants.description: Charming lakeside eatery overlooking Lake Voulismeni, committed to using only local Cretan ingredients. Known for traditional pitas and home-style cooking.

restaurants.name: Zygos Urban Garden
restaurants.baby_notes: Relaxed outdoor garden setting under trees by the lake. Shaded seating is ideal with baby. Good for lunch or drinks. Stylish but casual.
restaurants.description: Stylish outdoor garden restaurant next to Lake Voulismeni with tree-shaded seating, creative cocktails, and modern Mediterranean dishes.

restaurants.name: Migomis
restaurants.baby_notes: Cliffside fine dining with live piano. Romantic adults-oriented atmosphere. Consider for a special night out if hotel offers babysitting. Stunning panoramic views.
restaurants.description: Clifftop restaurant in a historic building with panoramic views of Agios Nikolaos, live piano, and creative Mediterranean cuisine.

accommodation.name: Daios Cove Luxury Resort & Villas
accommodation.room: Deluxe Sea View w/ Individual Pool
accommodation.dining_notes: Baby puree available at all restaurants and Mini Market

activities.name: Agios Nikolaos Archaeological Museum
activities.baby_notes: Small air-conditioned museum, good for a hot afternoon. Baby carrier works well. Quick visit with Minoan artifacts. Near Lake Voulismeni for a combined outing.
activities.description: Compact museum showcasing Minoan, Roman, and Byzantine artifacts from the Lassithi region including pottery, jewelry, and the famous Goddess of Myrtos.

activities.name: Boat Trip Around Mirabello Bay
activities.baby_notes: Choose a calmer bay cruise rather than open sea. Bring sun protection, hat, and shade for baby. May include swimming stops in calm coves. Check operator for life vests for infants.
activities.description: Scenic boat excursion around the Gulf of Mirabello with swimming stops in secluded coves, passing by Spinalonga and the dramatic coastline.

activities.name: Elounda to Plaka Coastal Walk
activities.hours: All day (best morning or late afternoon)
activities.baby_notes: Paved road along the coast, stroller-friendly. Stunning sea views the entire way. End in Plaka for lunch at a taverna. Can drive back or walk both ways. ~3km each way.
activities.description: Scenic coastal walk from Elounda village north to Plaka, with views of Spinalonga Island and the turquoise bay the entire route.

activities.name: Tholos Beach
activities.baby_notes: Small sheltered cove with very calm water. Sandy beach with shallow entry. Less crowded than Voulisma. Good alternative on busy days. ~25 min drive from Daios Cove.
activities.description: Pretty sheltered cove with golden sand and calm turquoise water, less crowded than the more famous nearby beaches.

restaurants.name: Piato
restaurants.baby_notes: Well-regarded restaurant with courteous service. Outdoor seating available. Creative dishes using local ingredients. Accommodating to families.
restaurants.description: Highly-rated restaurant in Agios Nikolaos center serving refined Greek cuisine with creative interpretations of local dishes.

restaurants.name: Chrysofyllis Taverna
restaurants.baby_notes: Budget-friendly traditional taverna. Generous portions of home-style Cretan food. Locals' favorite. Unpretentious and welcoming for families.
restaurants.description: No-frills traditional taverna beloved by locals for generous portions of authentic Cretan home cooking at very reasonable prices.

restaurants.name: Pelagos
restaurants.baby_notes: Near the harbor with outdoor seating. Fresh fish displayed on ice. Mediterranean breeze keeps it cool. Baby-friendly relaxed atmosphere.
restaurants.description: Well-established seafood restaurant near the harbor of Agios Nikolaos, known for its fresh fish display and expertly prepared Greek seafood dishes.

restaurants.name: Poulis Restaurant
restaurants.baby_notes: Located at Kitroplateia beach area. Tables near the waterfront. Baby can enjoy the sea view and breeze. Casual beachside dining.
restaurants.description: Waterfront restaurant at the popular Kitroplateia area serving fresh seafood and Mediterranean dishes with views of the small beach and open sea.

days.label: Crete

accommodation.name: Daios Cove Luxury Resort & Villas
accommodation.room: Deluxe Sea View w/ Individual Pool
accommodation.dining_notes: Baby puree available at all restaurants and Mini Market

activities.name: Agios Nikolaos Marina & Waterfront Stroll
activities.baby_notes: Wide flat promenade along the marina is excellent for strollers. Many ice cream shops and cafes for breaks. Small playground nearby.
activities.description: Walk along the charming marina with its bobbing boats, then along the waterfront promenade connecting to the town center and Lake Voulismeni.

activities.name: Katharo Plateau Drive
activities.baby_notes: Scenic drive on winding mountain road - baby will likely sleep in car seat. The plateau itself is flat and peaceful for a short walk. Stop at Kritsa on the way. ~40 min drive from Daios Cove.
activities.description: Dramatic drive up to 1100m elevation to a remote mountain plateau used for grazing. Stunning views of Mirabello Bay on the ascent.

activities.name: Cretan Aquarium (CretAquarium)
activities.baby_notes: Fully stroller-accessible, air-conditioned. Colorful fish tanks captivate even young babies. Changing facilities available. Good rainy-day option. ~45 min drive from Daios Cove.
activities.description: One of the largest aquariums in the Mediterranean with 60 tanks showcasing Mediterranean marine life including sharks, jellyfish, and sea turtles.

activities.name: Amazonas Park
activities.baby_notes: Small animal rescue park with shaded paths. Stroller-friendly. Monkeys, donkeys, goats, and birds engage young children. Relaxed pace. ~15 min drive from Daios Cove.
activities.description: Small family-run animal rescue park with monkeys, farm animals, and exotic birds in a natural hillside setting near Kritsa.

restaurants.name: Despina Taverna
restaurants.baby_notes: Seaside paradise with relaxed atmosphere. Fresh expertly crafted seafood. Book ahead as very popular. Outdoor terrace right by the water.
restaurants.description: Popular waterfront taverna in Elounda known for fresh seafood dishes, attentive service, and a lovely seaside setting.

restaurants.name: Thalassino Ageri
restaurants.baby_notes: Seaside terrace on the waterfront promenade. Renowned seafood. Tables are spacious enough for a stroller or high chair. Relaxed pace of service.
restaurants.description: Waterfront seafood restaurant along the Agios Nikolaos promenade, praised for its fresh fish, seafood platters, and beautiful sea views.

restaurants.name: Istron Bay Taverna
restaurants.baby_notes: Perfect lunch stop after Voulisma Beach. Shaded outdoor seating with bay views. Simple grilled fish and salads. Very laid-back. ~20 min drive from Daios Cove.
restaurants.description: Relaxed beachside taverna near Voulisma Beach serving fresh grilled fish, Cretan salads, and cold drinks with gorgeous bay views.

restaurants.name: Marilena Restaurant
restaurants.baby_notes: Laid-back village taverna with outdoor seating. Home-cooked Cretan food. Friendly service accommodating to families. Good value for money.
restaurants.description: Friendly village taverna in Elounda serving generous portions of traditional Cretan home cooking at honest prices.

notes: Last full day at Daios Cove

days.label: Crete → Zurich

checkout.notes: Check out early for 10:55 AM flight

car_rental.notes: Return rental car at HER airport before flight

notes: Return rental car at Heraklion airport
Hotel is at Zurich Airport - walk from terminal

activities.name: Swiss National Museum (Landesmuseum)
activities.baby_notes: Elevator access throughout, stroller-friendly galleries, free entry for children, baby changing facilities available
activities.description: Switzerland's most-visited cultural history museum, right next to Hauptbahnhof. Covers Swiss history from prehistory to present day.

activities.name: Kunsthaus Zürich
activities.cost: CHF 24 (free Wed for collection, free under 16)
activities.baby_notes: Strollers allowed in exhibition rooms; baby carriers recommended for some galleries. Cafe in entrance area is stroller-accessible.
activities.description: Switzerland's largest art museum with works by Picasso, Monet, Chagall, and Giacometti. The 2021 Chipperfield extension doubled the space.

activities.name: Old Town (Altstadt) Walking Tour
activities.address: Start at Hauptbahnhof, through Niederdorf and Lindenhof
activities.baby_notes: Mostly flat cobblestone streets, some steep sections near Lindenhof. Best with a sturdy stroller or baby carrier. Many cafes for nursing breaks.
activities.description: Wander medieval lanes through Niederdorf, discover hidden squares, guild houses, and street fountains. The heart of historic Zurich.

activities.name: Lindenhof
activities.baby_notes: Shaded hilltop park with benches. Reached via stairs or gentle slope from Bahnhofstrasse side. Great spot for a rest with baby.
activities.description: Historic hilltop park with panoramic views over the Limmat river, Old Town rooftops, and university. Site of a Roman fort.

activities.name: Fraumünster Church
activities.baby_notes: Stroller access available. Quiet interior — baby carrier preferred to keep noise low. Quick visit works well with infant.
activities.description: 13th-century church famous for its stunning stained-glass windows by Marc Chagall and a rosette by Augusto Giacometti.

activities.name: Lake Zurich Promenade
activities.baby_notes: Flat, paved lakeside path perfect for strollers. Benches everywhere, public restrooms at Bürkliplatz. Great for morning or evening walks.
activities.description: Scenic lakeside stroll along Lake Zurich with views of the Alps on clear days. Pass parks, sculptures, and boat docks.

activities.name: Chapel Bridge (Kapellbrücke)
activities.baby_notes: Flat wooden bridge, stroller accessible, covered from rain. Great photo spot with Water Tower.
activities.description: Europe's oldest covered wooden bridge, dating from 1333. Spans the Reuss River with 17th-century paintings in the roof gables and the iconic octagonal Water Tower.

activities.name: Lion Monument (Löwendenkmal)
activities.baby_notes: Short walk from Old Town, paved path to viewing area. Stroller accessible. Quiet pond setting good for babies.
activities.description: Mark Twain called it the most mournful piece of stone in the world. Carved into a rock face in 1821, it commemorates the Swiss Guards who fell during the French Revolution.

activities.name: Lake Lucerne Waterfront Promenade
activities.baby_notes: Flat, wide paved promenade perfect for strollers. Benches everywhere for nursing breaks. Stunning Alpine views across the lake.
activities.description: The lakefront promenade stretching from the train station along Schweizerhofquai offers panoramic views of Lake Lucerne, Mount Pilatus, and Mount Rigi. Ideal for a leisurely stroll.

activities.name: Old Town (Altstadt) Stroll
activities.baby_notes: Nearly car-free cobblestone streets. Mostly flat with some gentle slopes. Many cafes for rest stops. Weinmarkt fountain square is a nice pause spot.
activities.description: Lucerne's medieval Old Town on the north bank of the Reuss features painted frescoed facades, charming squares like Weinmarkt and Hirschenplatz, and boutique shopping streets.

activities.name: Spreuer Bridge (Spreuerbrücke)
activities.baby_notes: Flat covered wooden bridge, stroller accessible. Less crowded than Chapel Bridge.
activities.description: A 15th-century covered wooden footbridge featuring Kaspar Meglinger's 'Dance of Death' paintings from the 1600s in the roof gables. Quieter alternative to the Chapel Bridge.

activities.name: Jesuit Church (Jesuitenkirche)
activities.baby_notes: Ground-level entrance, spacious interior. Keep baby quiet during services. Stroller fits easily.
activities.description: Switzerland's first large Baroque church, built 1666-1677. The stunning pink-and-white stucco interior is one of the finest Baroque interiors in the country.

activities.name: Lake Lucerne Panoramic Boat Cruise (1 hour)
activities.address: SGV Terminal, Werftestrasse 5, 6002 Luzern (directly across from Lucerne HB)
activities.baby_notes: Strollers roll right on board. Indoor and outdoor seating. Smooth ride suitable for infants. On-board cafe for warm drinks. Baby under 6 rides free.
activities.description: A scenic one-hour panoramic cruise on Lake Lucerne with views of Mount Pilatus, Mount Rigi, and the surrounding Alps. Departs from the pier right across from the train station.

activities.name: Musegg Wall (Museggmauer)
activities.baby_notes: Narrow steep tower stairs are NOT stroller accessible. Best with a baby carrier if you want to climb a tower. The wall-top walkway is narrow. Consider skipping towers and just viewing from below.
activities.description: Lucerne's remarkably preserved medieval city wall stretches 870 meters with nine watchtowers. The Zyt Tower houses the city's oldest clock, which chimes one minute before all others.

restaurants.name: Confiserie Sprüngli
restaurants.baby_notes: Famous Luxemburgerli macarons — easy to eat one-handed while holding baby. Indoor seating can be tight; ground floor counter service works well.
restaurants.description: Zurich's iconic luxury confiserie since 1836. Famous for Luxemburgerli macarons, truffles, and elegant pastries. A must-visit chocolate experience.

restaurants.name: Zeughauskeller
restaurants.baby_notes: Very spacious beer-hall seating with long tables — easy to park a stroller. High chairs available. Bustling atmosphere covers baby noise.
restaurants.description: Historic 15th-century armoury turned restaurant serving Swiss classics: Zürcher Geschnetzeltes, sausages, rösti, and schnitzel since 1926.

restaurants.name: Swiss Chuchi (Hotel Adler)
restaurants.baby_notes: Outdoor terrace on the Niederdorf pedestrian lane — great for strollers. High chairs available. Note: keep baby away from hot fondue pot.
restaurants.description: The Old Town's premier fondue destination since 1953. Nine fondue varieties, raclette, and classic Swiss dishes in a charming Niederdorf setting.

restaurants.name: Restaurant Kronenhalle
restaurants.baby_notes: Upscale but welcoming. High chairs available. Best at lunch when atmosphere is more relaxed. Original Chagall and Miró on the walls.
restaurants.description: Legendary Zurich institution since 1924 with original art by Chagall, Miró, and Matisse on the walls. Famous for Zürcher Geschnetzeltes and rösti.

restaurants.name: Haus Hiltl
restaurants.baby_notes: Buffet style — perfect for parents since you serve yourself quickly. Spacious, high chairs available, baby changing facilities. Very relaxed vibe.
restaurants.description: The world's oldest vegetarian restaurant (Guinness record, since 1898). Over 100 dishes from the daily buffet — pay by weight. Indian, Asian, Swiss options.

restaurants.name: Café & Confiserie 1842 (Schober)
restaurants.baby_notes: Multiple cozy rooms with space for strollers. Famous hot chocolate is a treat for parents. Historic Old Town setting with calm daytime atmosphere.
restaurants.description: Zurich's oldest cafe (est. 1842) in the heart of Niederdorf. Legendary hot chocolate, handmade pastries, and ornate Belle Époque interiors.

restaurants.name: Soul Chicken
restaurants.baby_notes: Casual relaxed atmosphere, no fuss dining. Compact space so stroller may need folding at peak times. Good for a quick satisfying meal.
restaurants.description: Beloved local spot famous for crispy fried chicken served in a basket with garlic or soul sauce and fresh baguette. Cozy artistic interior with a loyal following.

restaurants.name: Rathaus Brauerei
restaurants.baby_notes: High chairs available, kids menu with coloring sheets, baby changing table. Riverside outdoor terrace with Chapel Bridge view is ideal for families.
restaurants.description: Lucerne's own craft brewpub right on the Reuss River with views of Chapel Bridge. Serves house-brewed beers alongside hearty Swiss and Bavarian dishes.

restaurants.name: tibits Luzern
restaurants.address: Zentralstrasse 1, 6003 Luzern (inside Lucerne train station, 1st floor)
restaurants.baby_notes: High chairs available, baby changing table. Buffet style lets you grab exactly what you need quickly. Located in the train station -- perfect for arrival or departure meal.
restaurants.description: Popular vegetarian and vegan buffet restaurant inside Lucerne train station. Over 40 freshly prepared dishes from salads to hot mains, paid by plate weight.

restaurants.name: Wirtshaus Galliker
restaurants.baby_notes: Relaxed traditional atmosphere, spacious wooden tables. Reservation recommended. A genuine local institution -- not touristy.
restaurants.description: A fourth-generation family-run restaurant serving traditional Swiss home cooking since the 1880s. Known for classics like Rösti, bratwurst, cordon bleu, and seasonal specialties.

restaurants.name: Restaurant Ammos
restaurants.baby_notes: High chairs available, riverside terrace is spacious for strollers. Mezze sharing plates work well when dining with a baby on your lap.
restaurants.description: Authentic Greek restaurant on the banks of the Reuss River with a terrace overlooking the Chapel Bridge. Known for fresh mezze, grilled meats, and seafood.

restaurants.name: Bierliebe and Friends
restaurants.baby_notes: Baby chairs available, kids menu, changing table, toys upstairs. Right next to Chapel Bridge. The Raclette Burger is large enough to share.
restaurants.description: Family-friendly burger restaurant next to Chapel Bridge with craft beers on tap. Popular for their Raclette Burger and relaxed riverside vibe.

activities.name: Zoo Zürich
activities.baby_notes: Fully stroller accessible with paved paths. Baby changing stations available. Masoala Rainforest hall is warm and great for babies. Nursing rooms on site.
activities.description: Award-winning zoo with the Masoala Rainforest hall, Kaeng Krachan elephant park, and Australian outback exhibit. Reach via tram 6 from HB.

activities.name: Old Botanical Garden (Alter Botanischer Garten)
activities.baby_notes: Flat paths through gardens, shaded areas under old trees. A peaceful escape near Hauptbahnhof for feeding or nap breaks.
activities.description: Dating to 1837, this tranquil garden features rare trees, medicinal herb beds, and a historic palm house. A hidden gem steps from Bahnhofstrasse.

activities.name: Bahnhofstrasse Shopping Stroll
activities.baby_notes: Wide, flat, car-free boulevard ideal for strollers. Sprüngli and other shops provide pleasant rest stops along the way.
activities.description: One of the world's most famous shopping streets, stretching 1.4 km from Hauptbahnhof to Lake Zurich. Luxury boutiques, watch shops, and chocolate.

activities.name: Limmat River Walk
activities.baby_notes: Flat riverside path. Many cafes with outdoor seating line the quay. Good stroller terrain with views of guild houses and churches.
activities.description: Walk along the Limmat river through the heart of Zurich, passing the Rathaus, Grossmünster, and colorful guild houses reflected in the water.

activities.name: Lindt Home of Chocolate
activities.baby_notes: Modern facility with elevator access and stroller-friendly paths. Baby carrier recommended for the museum tour. Chocolate shop at end is stroller-ok.
activities.description: Interactive chocolate museum with the world's tallest chocolate fountain, tasting stations, and a massive Lindt shop. Book time slots in advance.

activities.name: Bürkliplatz & Boat Dock
activities.baby_notes: Flat open plaza at the lake. Optional short boat cruise — strollers allowed on boats. Sat flea market is fun to browse. Public restrooms nearby.
activities.description: Where Bahnhofstrasse meets Lake Zurich. Watch boats, enjoy lake views, or take a short cruise. Saturday flea market in summer.

activities.name: Chapel Bridge (Kapellbrücke)
activities.baby_notes: Flat wooden bridge, stroller accessible, covered from rain. Great photo spot with Water Tower.
activities.description: Europe's oldest covered wooden bridge, dating from 1333. Spans the Reuss River with 17th-century paintings in the roof gables and the iconic octagonal Water Tower.

activities.name: Lion Monument (Löwendenkmal)
activities.baby_notes: Short walk from Old Town, paved path to viewing area. Stroller accessible. Quiet pond setting good for babies.
activities.description: Mark Twain called it the most mournful piece of stone in the world. Carved into a rock face in 1821, it commemorates the Swiss Guards who fell during the French Revolution.

activities.name: Lake Lucerne Waterfront Promenade
activities.baby_notes: Flat, wide paved promenade perfect for strollers. Benches everywhere for nursing breaks. Stunning Alpine views across the lake.
activities.description: The lakefront promenade stretching from the train station along Schweizerhofquai offers panoramic views of Lake Lucerne, Mount Pilatus, and Mount Rigi. Ideal for a leisurely stroll.

activities.name: Old Town (Altstadt) Stroll
activities.baby_notes: Nearly car-free cobblestone streets. Mostly flat with some gentle slopes. Many cafes for rest stops. Weinmarkt fountain square is a nice pause spot.
activities.description: Lucerne's medieval Old Town on the north bank of the Reuss features painted frescoed facades, charming squares like Weinmarkt and Hirschenplatz, and boutique shopping streets.

activities.name: Spreuer Bridge (Spreuerbrücke)
activities.baby_notes: Flat covered wooden bridge, stroller accessible. Less crowded than Chapel Bridge.
activities.description: A 15th-century covered wooden footbridge featuring Kaspar Meglinger's 'Dance of Death' paintings from the 1600s in the roof gables. Quieter alternative to the Chapel Bridge.

activities.name: Jesuit Church (Jesuitenkirche)
activities.baby_notes: Ground-level entrance, spacious interior. Keep baby quiet during services. Stroller fits easily.
activities.description: Switzerland's first large Baroque church, built 1666-1677. The stunning pink-and-white stucco interior is one of the finest Baroque interiors in the country.

activities.name: Lake Lucerne Panoramic Boat Cruise (1 hour)
activities.address: SGV Terminal, Werftestrasse 5, 6002 Luzern (directly across from Lucerne HB)
activities.baby_notes: Strollers roll right on board. Indoor and outdoor seating. Smooth ride suitable for infants. On-board cafe for warm drinks. Baby under 6 rides free.
activities.description: A scenic one-hour panoramic cruise on Lake Lucerne with views of Mount Pilatus, Mount Rigi, and the surrounding Alps. Departs from the pier right across from the train station.

activities.name: Musegg Wall (Museggmauer)
activities.baby_notes: Narrow steep tower stairs are NOT stroller accessible. Best with a baby carrier if you want to climb a tower. The wall-top walkway is narrow. Consider skipping towers and just viewing from below.
activities.description: Lucerne's remarkably preserved medieval city wall stretches 870 meters with nine watchtowers. The Zyt Tower houses the city's oldest clock, which chimes one minute before all others.

restaurants.name: Café Bar Odeon
restaurants.baby_notes: Art Nouveau interior is spacious. Best for breakfast or lunch when quieter. Terrace has outdoor seating facing Bellevueplatz and the lake.
restaurants.description: Legendary 1911 Art Nouveau grand cafe at Bellevueplatz where Lenin and Einstein once sat. Great for coffee, breakfast, or a light lunch.

restaurants.name: Tibits Seefeld
restaurants.baby_notes: Self-service buffet is quick and easy for parents. Spacious modern space near Bellevue tram stop. Stroller-friendly. Good for a fast healthy meal.
restaurants.description: Popular vegetarian buffet chain — over 50 items daily, pay by weight. Fresh, colorful, and quick. Near Bellevueplatz, perfect post-lake-walk refuel.

restaurants.name: Raclette Stube
restaurants.baby_notes: Small cozy restaurant — call ahead to request stroller-friendly table. Intimate atmosphere; early dinner slot recommended with baby.
restaurants.description: Dedicated raclette restaurant in the Old Town. Traditional Swiss raclette with potatoes, pickles, and onions — a quintessential alpine experience.

restaurants.name: Sternen Grill
restaurants.baby_notes: Outdoor standing/seating area — easy with a stroller. Quick service, no wait. Zurich's best bratwurst is a fast no-fuss meal for busy parents.
restaurants.description: Zurich's most beloved bratwurst stand since 1963. The Sternen Grill sausage with bürli bread is a local institution near Bellevueplatz.

restaurants.name: Markthalle im Viadukt
restaurants.baby_notes: Wide aisles, casual atmosphere, multiple food options so both parents can eat what they want. Stroller-friendly throughout. Baby changing available.
restaurants.description: Artisan food market under the railway viaduct arches in Zurich West. Fresh produce, cheese, bread, Asian food, and specialty goods — great for grazing.

restaurants.name: Bauernschänke
restaurants.baby_notes: Rustic atmosphere with wooden interiors. High chairs available. Traditional Swiss comfort food — generous portions good for sharing.
restaurants.description: Traditional Swiss-German restaurant on charming Rindermarkt square in the Old Town. Hearty classics: Kalbsgeschnetzeltes, rösti, and Swiss sausages.

restaurants.name: Soul Chicken
restaurants.baby_notes: Casual relaxed atmosphere, no fuss dining. Compact space so stroller may need folding at peak times. Good for a quick satisfying meal.
restaurants.description: Beloved local spot famous for crispy fried chicken served in a basket with garlic or soul sauce and fresh baguette. Cozy artistic interior with a loyal following.

restaurants.name: Rathaus Brauerei
restaurants.baby_notes: High chairs available, kids menu with coloring sheets, baby changing table. Riverside outdoor terrace with Chapel Bridge view is ideal for families.
restaurants.description: Lucerne's own craft brewpub right on the Reuss River with views of Chapel Bridge. Serves house-brewed beers alongside hearty Swiss and Bavarian dishes.

restaurants.name: tibits Luzern
restaurants.address: Zentralstrasse 1, 6003 Luzern (inside Lucerne train station, 1st floor)
restaurants.baby_notes: High chairs available, baby changing table. Buffet style lets you grab exactly what you need quickly. Located in the train station -- perfect for arrival or departure meal.
restaurants.description: Popular vegetarian and vegan buffet restaurant inside Lucerne train station. Over 40 freshly prepared dishes from salads to hot mains, paid by plate weight.

restaurants.baby_notes: Relaxed traditional atmosphere, spacious wooden tables. Reservation recommended. A genuine local institution -- not touristy.
restaurants.description: A fourth-generation family-run restaurant serving traditional Swiss home cooking since the 1880s. Known for classics like Rösti, bratwurst, cordon bleu, and seasonal specialties.

restaurants.baby_notes: High chairs available, riverside terrace is spacious for strollers. Mezze sharing plates work well when dining with a baby on your lap.
restaurants.description: Authentic Greek restaurant on the banks of the Reuss River with a terrace overlooking the Chapel Bridge. Known for fresh mezze, grilled meats, and seafood.

restaurants.name: Bierliebe and Friends
restaurants.baby_notes: Baby chairs available, kids menu, changing table, toys upstairs. Right next to Chapel Bridge. The Raclette Burger is large enough to share.
restaurants.description: Family-friendly burger restaurant next to Chapel Bridge with craft beers on tap. Popular for their Raclette Burger and relaxed riverside vibe.
